steps.title: Use the factored form
steps.content:
The graph has an $x$-intercept wherever $f(x)=0$.
$$x(x-10)^2(x-11)^3=0$$
Each distinct factor that can equal zero gives one distinct $x$-intercept.

steps.title: Count distinct roots
steps.content:
The roots come from the factors $x$, $(x-10)^2$, and $(x-11)^3$.
$$x=0$$
$$x=10$$
$$x=11$$
There are $3$ distinct roots.

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Graph the polynomial and count the distinct places where it meets the $x$-axis. There are $3$ such intercepts. The answer is D.
